texto: He remembered hearing Karl tell James once that it was hard for people to ever know what they really looked like. Reflections in mirrors weren’t accurate, Karl said, because when you stared at yourself in a mirror, you subconsciously composed your face in a way that wasn’t your natural expression.Marvin wondered it that was true when you were with strangers too. Maybe you only looked like your true self with the people you loved. And maybe that was a face you yourself hardly ever got to see…
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Elise Broach: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “He remembered hearing Karl tell James once that it was hard for people to ever know what they rea…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, face, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de love e mirror.

Maybe you only looked like your true self with the people you loved. And maybe that was a face you yourself hardly ever got to see…

texto: My dad always says, some people will treat you badly and you can't help that. But how you handle it and how it makes you feel, that's up to you.
explicacao: Citação de Elise Broach sobre temas ligados a motivacao. “My dad always says, some people will treat you badly and you can't help that.
